t_ch: 那只雏鸟还小，连羽毛都没长齐，也不会唱歌。我们发现时，它落在草丛中奄奄一息，似乎是被父母遗弃了。
t_en: That baby bird was tiny, it didn't even have all of its feathers, and it couldn't sing. When we found it, it was already on its last breath, having fallen into a shrub — probably abandoned by its parents.

t_ch: 我们当即决定为它搭建一座鸟巢。可思来想去，那年的冬天很冷，庭院夜间的风很大，还有许多毒虫和野兽出没……
t_en: We decided to build a nest for it right there and then. However, thinking back, that winter was unusually cold, with fierce winds at night in the yard, not to mention the many poisonous bugs and wild beasts in the vicinity...

t_ch: 毫无疑问，如果将雏鸟留在庭院，它绝无可能坚持到春天。于是我提议带它回去，放在窗前的木架上，并拜托大人造个鸟笼。
t_en: It was clear that if we left the fledgling in the yard, it stood no chance of surviving until spring. So, I suggested we take it inside, place it on the shelf by the window, and asked the adults to fashion a cage for it.

t_ch: 我们约定，等到它能依靠自己的力量展翅翱翔时，再将它放回天空。可遗憾的是，我们万万没有想到，这只鸟儿的命运早在那之前就已经注定——
t_en: We decided that when it regained its strength enough to spread its wings, we would release it back into the wild. The tragic part — something that we'd never considered — was that this bird's fate had already been determined long before this moment...

t_ch: 它落得何种下场，只在我们的一念之间。
t_en: Its destiny was determined by our momentary whim.

t_ch: 现在，我将把选择的权利交给各位。面对此情此景，你们会如何抉择？
t_en: Now, I pass the power of choice to you all. Faced with this situation, what choice would you make?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>是采取我们最初的方案，用软垫在原地为小谐乐鸽打造鸟巢？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Stick to the original plan, and build a nest with soft net where the Charmony Dove fell?</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>还是选择为它打造鸟笼，在温暖的屋宇中精心饲养鸟儿？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Or build a cage for it, and feed it, giving it the utmost care from within the warmth of a home?</color>

t_ch: 我期待你们的答案。
t_en: I eagerly await your answer.

t_ch: 那家伙没脑地抛给我们这么个问题，到底是几个意思？不过答一答也不是不行……
t_en: That guy just casually throws this kind of question at us? What exactly is his deal? But fine, I'll answer I guess...

t_ch: 如果是我的话…应该会选择<color=#dbc291ff>给小谐乐鸽打造鸟笼</color>吧？
t_en: If it were me... I guess I'd choose to <color=#dbc291ff>build a cage for the little Charmony Dove</color>.

t_ch: 毕竟如果把它留在原地，肯定会被野兽伤到的…那样的话，小鸟也太可怜了。
t_en: After all, leaving it there, it's bound to get hurt by wild animals or something... And that'd just be too sad.

t_ch: 暂时还看不透他的用意…如果只看题面，我应该会选择<color=#dbc291ff>给小谐乐鸽打造鸟笼</color>……
t_en: I can't decipher his intentions right now... but based solely on the question, I would probably choose to <color=#dbc291ff>build that dove a cage</color>...

t_ch: 就算要放回天空，也得它能够展翅翱翔才行。如果把它留在原地…恐怕它再也不会拥有飞上天空的机会了。
t_en: Even if I was gonna release it back into the sky, it'd have to be strong enough to fly first. If I left it where I found it... I fear it'd never get the chance to fly ever again.

t_ch: 看起来他确实不打算囚禁我们。如果只是问答游戏，陪他过过招也无妨。
t_en: It looks like he really has no intention of imprisoning us. If it's just a quiz, I suppose it's fine to humor him.

t_ch: 回到问题本身，我个人会选择<color=#dbc291ff>给小谐乐鸽打造鸟笼</color>。
t_en: Back to the question: I would personally choose to <color=#dbc291ff>build the little Charmony Dove a cage</color>.

t_ch: 没什么特别的理由。我只是觉得每一只雏鸟都有飞上天空的权利…但如果它活不到那个时候，就无从谈起了。
t_en: No special reason. I do think that a fledgling should have the right to fly in the sky... But if it can't even live to that point, then there's nothing to talk about to begin with.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>要采取最初的方案，用软垫在原地为小谐乐鸽打造鸟巢吗？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Stick to the original plan, and build a nest with soft net where the Charmony Dove fell?</color>

t_ch: 做出选择。
t_en: Make the decision.

t_ch: 再想想看。
t_en: Think it over some more.

t_ch: 有趣。既然你心意已决，就让我来揭晓这一选择会为那只雏鸟带来何种命运吧。
t_en: Interesting. Since you've made up your mind, allow me to reveal what fate this choice will bring to the fledgling.

t_ch: 就我观察到的结果，至少有三种以小型鸟类为食的野兽在那片庭院中出没：伏氏镰尾蝎、阿斯德纳狼獾，还有亨廷顿翼颈蛇……
t_en: From what I've observed, there are at least three predators in that yard that prey on small birds: The Vossickle Scorpion, Asdana Wolverine, and the Huntington Winged-Snake...

t_ch: 尽管无法伤及人类，但这些生物在小小的一方庭院里仍是上位掠夺者。在这种地方，等待那只小谐乐鸽的命运只有一种——痛苦的死亡。
t_en: Even if they shy away from humans, these animals are still near-apex predators in a fenced location like a yard. In such a location, only one fate awaits that little Charmony Dove: A painful death.

t_ch: 对于你做出的选择…我深表遗憾。接下来，让我们进入第二个抉择吧。这次…是一位逐梦客的故事。
t_en: As for the choice you made... I am deeply sorry. Now, let us advance to the second decision. This time... it's the story of a Dreamchaser.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>要选择为小谐乐鸽打造鸟笼，在温暖的屋宇中精心饲养鸟儿吗？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Or build a cage for it, and feed it, giving it the utmost care from within the warmth of a home?</color>

t_ch: 做出选择。
t_en: Make the decision.

t_ch: 再想想看。
t_en: Think it over some more.

t_ch: 很高兴看见你和当年的我们做出了同样的选择。既然你心意已决，就让我来揭晓这一选择的结果吧。
t_en: I'm happy to see that you made a choice similar to ours. If your mind is made up, let me reveal the outcome of this choice.

t_ch: 我们认真将它抚养大，每天都备好精致的食料，还为它梳理羽毛。后来，在知更鸟离开匹诺康尼的那天，我们打开笼子的门，让它回到天空。
t_en: We passionately nursed it back to health, preparing only the best food for it every day. We even preened its feathers. Later, on the day that Robin left Penacony, we opened the cage door and let it fly back into the sky.

t_ch: 我在窗前看了它很久，大概三天左右吧。在那漫长的三天里，小谐乐鸽一次又一次地试图展开翅膀，飞向天空，又落向地面…周而复始。
t_en: I watched it for a long while by the window, probably about three or so days. In those three long days, the little Charmony Dove tried again and again to spread its wings to fly into the sky, but fell to the ground... only to keep trying.

t_ch: 最后，在第一百三十七次尝试时，它终于成功了。但这一次尝试并不圆满…在摇摇晃晃地飞行了一段时间后，它因无法准确掌握空气的流向而坠落在地。
t_en: Finally, on the hundred and thirty-seventh attempt, it succeeded. But its attempt did not go perfectly... After flying unsteadily for a while, it fell to the ground, unable to grasp the direction of the air currents.

t_ch: 这一坠彻底摔断了它的翅膀。它在我的怀中苦苦挣扎，却无济于事，最终迎来痛苦的死亡。而我们的悉心照料、寄予的关爱和厚望，在这一瞬间都成了葬送它的推手。
t_en: The fall shattered its wings. It writhed helplessly in my embrace, but it was all for naught, finally succumbing to a painful demise. And in that instant, our tender care, our given love and hopes... they all became the inevitable push that sent it to its death.

t_ch: 对于我们做出的选择…我深表遗憾。接下来，让我们进入第二个抉择吧。这次…是一位逐梦客的故事。
t_en: I deeply regret the choices we made... Next, let us head to the second decision. This time... it's the story of a Dreamchaser.

t_ch: 这则故事发生在我任职「铎音」期间。铎音是橡木家系特有的职位，负责聆听梦境居民的困惑和迷茫，给予他们相应的指导。
t_en: This story happened when I was appointed as Bronze Melodia — a position exclusive to the Oak Family, charged with listening to the problems and vexations of Dreamscape residents, and providing them with the relevant guidance.

t_ch: 也正是在那段时间，我得以听取来自梦境各方的声音：有欢喜、有忧愁、有傲慢、有悔恨…复杂的人性编织起世界，而我有幸窥见其中一斑。
t_en: It was during that period that I had the opportunity to hear voices from all corners of the Dreamscape: Joy, sorrow, arrogance, regret... The complex tapestry of human nature that formed the world, and I was fortunate to catch a glimpse of it.

t_ch: 那是一位逐梦客，也是一名偷渡犯。和其他所有人一样，他怀着对美好生活的向往来到匹诺康尼，只是他付出的代价对于凡人而言…也足以称作「倾尽一切」。
t_en: He was a Dreamchaser, and an illegal stowaway. Just like the rest of them, he came to Penacony in search of a better life, except that, to most people, the price he paid... I suppose you could say it was "everything."

t_ch: 他这么对我说，「把家里能卖的全都卖了」——房子，土地，还有两个孩子…他说自己无力抚养他们，做奴隶至少还能混口饭吃。
t_en: He told me "I sold everything I could at home." — the house, the land, even his two children... He said he could not afford to raise them, and that at least they could eat if they lived as slaves.

t_ch: 他也做好了计划，准备发家致富后就把孩子赎回，共享匹诺康尼的美梦。只可惜他的偷渡计划有些笨拙，被那群冥顽不灵的猎犬们嗅出了气息。
t_en: He had a plan in place: He would buy back his children once he had made his fortune, and enjoy Penacony's beautiful dream with them. Alas, his plan to smuggle himself was somewhat clumsy — and he was sniffed out by those pigheaded Hounds.

t_ch: 听过这位逐梦客的故事，我当即请求猎犬家系停止追捕，这样他便能安心生活。可惜我的眼界太浅，不曾想这自以为是的善举竟在日后酿成了苦果……
t_en: After hearing the Dreamchaser's story, I immediately appealed to the Bloodhound Family to cease their pursuit. That way, at least he could live peacefully. But I was still too naive to the ways of the world — I did not anticipate that what I thought was a kind gesture would later lead to dire consequences...

t_ch: 答案稍后公布。现在，我希望各位做出选择——
t_en: I'll tell you the outcome soon. For now, I'd like you all to make a choice—

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>是做出和我一样的决定，说服猎犬家系停止搜查，以便那位逐梦客能够生存下去，实现他的愿望？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Will you do as I did, and try to convince the Bloodhound Family to stop their pursuit, so that the Dreamchaser may live peacefully and realize his wishes?</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>还是保持沉默，任凭他在猎犬的追踪下苟延残喘，直到注定的裁决来临？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Or will you remain silent, leaving him to languish while the Hounds are hot on his heels, until his inevitable judgment arrives?</color>

t_ch: 期待各位的选择，也许能够扭转那桩悲剧也说不定。
t_en: I look forward to everyone's decisions. Who knows, perhaps they might even alter the outcome of this tragedy.

t_ch: 看来偷渡客在匹诺康尼真的很常见啊…但故事里的这家伙，我觉得一点都不值得同情。
t_en: It seems illegal stowaways are really quite common on Penacony... But that guy in the story, I don't think he deserves any sympathy at all.

t_ch: 他为了追梦，竟然连孩子都卖了欸…就算有赎回的打算，也太不负责任了吧。
t_en: He sold his kids to chase a dream... Even if he intended to go back for them, it's still insanely irresponsible.

t_ch: 这么一想，就只有一种选择了：<color=#dbc291ff>让猎犬家系的人送他回去</color>！这人一定得受到惩罚！
t_en: With that thought, there is only one choice: <color=#dbc291ff>Let the Bloodhounds send him back home!</color> This person deserves to be punished!

t_ch: 这个问题…和雏鸟的故事有些内在联系，而这联系就是星期日想要说服我们的突破口……
t_en: This question... surely it has some connection to the baby bird story — and this connection is precisely the breakthrough Sunday aims to use to persuade us...

t_ch: …我应该会选择<color=#dbc291ff>请求猎犬家系停止追捕</color>。
t_en: ...I'd probably choose to <color=#dbc291ff>ask the Bloodhounds to cease their pursuit</color>.

t_ch: 逐梦客的故事啊…本着善意，我应该会<color=#dbc291ff>请求猎犬家系停止追捕</color>，拉他一把。
t_en: A Dreamchaser story... If I acted out of kindness, I would probably <color=#dbc291ff>ask the Bloodhounds to stop their pursuit</color> and lend him a hand.

t_ch: 不过，这个选择究竟会酿成什么样的「苦果」？我想，星期日一定是通过这件事深刻认识到了「以强援弱」的局限性吧。
t_en: But, what cruel repercussion would this choice result in? I think, Sunday must have been deeply impressed by the limitations of "the strong defending the weak" through this incident.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>要保持沉默，任凭他在猎犬的追踪下苟延残喘，直到注定的裁决来临吗？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Or will you remain silent, leaving him to languish while the Hounds are hot on his heels, until his inevitable judgment arrives?</color>

t_ch: 做出选择。
t_en: Make the decision.

t_ch: 再想想看。
t_en: Think it over some more.

t_ch: 看来你和我一样，也在思考另一种选择能否带来更好的结局。但很可惜，他的下场恐怕只会更加凄惨。
t_en: It seems you, like me, are pondering whether a different choice could have led to a better outcome. Sadly, his fate would only be more tragic.

t_ch: 假设他自始至终都没被抓到，那一定会因精神错乱而死。偷渡客的入梦手段大多非比寻常，不像酒店那样完善。想在梦境中生活根本是天方夜谭。
t_en: Say he never gets caught, he would only die from delirium. The methods with which illegal stowaways enter dreams are unorthodox, not flawless like the hotel's. Living in the Dreamscape would be a mere pipe dream.

t_ch: 如果他被抓到了，猎犬们会网开一面吗？答案是不可能。他们无法承担相应的后果，也自然不会冒险给予援助。
t_en: Should he be apprehended, could the Hounds afford to turn a blind eye? The answer is a definitive no. They couldn't bear the resulting consequences, and thus, wouldn't dare extend a helping hand.

t_ch: 对于这一抉择，我再次深表遗憾。接下来就是第三次，也是最后一次抉择了——
t_en: As to your choice, I once again offer my heartfelt apologies. Next comes the third and final decision—

t_ch: 而这次的故事…属于我本人。
t_en: And the story this time... is my own.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>要说服猎犬家系停止搜查，以便那位逐梦客能够生存下去，实现他的愿望吗？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Will you try to convince the Bloodhound Family to stop their pursuit, so that the Dreamchaser may live peacefully and realize his wishes?</color>

t_ch: 做出选择。
t_en: Make the decision.

t_ch: 再想想看。
t_en: Think it over some more.

t_ch: 看到你做出相同的决定，我深表荣幸。出于尊重，就告诉你我当年的决定招来了怎样的恶果吧。
t_en: I'm honored to witness you arriving at the same decision. Out of respect, I'll share with you the dire consequences that my choice back then brought about.

t_ch: 先说结果：他取得了巨大的成功。摆脱追捕后，他做了几年生意，很快跻身名流，平步青云。尽管不及老奥帝那样的大富翁，但也算是出类拔萃的风流人物。
t_en: First, the outcome: He attained major success. After avoiding capture, he ran a business for a few years, very quickly making a name for himself, elevating his status. He might not have become a tycoon like Old Oti, but he was considered a character of excellent repute.

t_ch: 那么他实现最初的愿望了吗？没有。我最后一次见他是在现实中，猎犬们正要将他永久驱逐，而我则是随行的铎音。使命很简单——聆听罪犯的忏悔。
t_en: Now then, did he realize the wish he set out to achieve? No. The last time I saw him was in the real world, where the Hounds were going to permanently exile him, and I was the accompanying Bronze Melodia. The mission was simple: Listen to the criminal's repentance.

t_ch: 他告诉我，自己是为了篡夺苜蓿草家主的地位，意图谋反才沦落至此。当我问及他的两个孩子，他却反问我：「什么孩子？」
t_en: He told me the reason he was in this predicament was because he conspired to usurp the head of the Alfalfa Family. When I asked him about his two children, he instead responded with a question: "What children?"

t_ch: 到头来，我心向「同谐」，冒险而为的善举没有任何意义，反成了恶行，造就了一位可悲的压迫者，还有千千万万被压迫的人……
t_en: In the end, my heart aligned with the Harmony, and the good deed I dared to undertake held no value, turning instead into a wrongdoing. It created a lamentable oppressor and countless oppressed individuals...

t_ch: 对于这一抉择，我再次深表遗憾。接下来就是第三次，也是最后一次抉择了——
t_en: As to your choice, I once again offer my heartfelt apologies. Next comes the third and final decision—

t_ch: 而这次的故事…属于我本人。
t_en: And the story this time... is my own.

t_ch: 这则故事发生在我就任橡木家主当天。那时歌斐木先生已成了如今的梦主，应他要求，我们进行了一场私人对谈。
t_en: This story happened the day I was appointed the Oak Family Head. At that time, Mr. Gopher Wood was the current Dreammaster, and as per his wish, we had a private conversation.

t_ch: 令我诧异的是，梦主只为我捎来了一封信。他让我读读信中的内容，而那信件…来自我的妹妹。
t_en: What surprised me was that the Dreammaster had only come to deliver a letter to me. He let me read its contents. It was a letter... from my sister.

t_ch: 信中无非是日常的寒暄，捎带她游历诸界的种种见闻。正当我心生疑惑，这封信与会谈有什么关系时，梦主开口了。
t_en: The letter contained the usual pleasantries, anecdotes from her travels — nothing out of the ordinary. Just as I started wondering how this letter related to our discussion, the Dreammaster began to speak.

t_ch: 「你知道么，这封信出自何人之手？」
t_en: "Do you know who wrote this letter?"

t_ch: 「当然是家妹的手笔。梦主为何要为我兄妹的日常琐事登门拜访？」
t_en: "My sister, of course. But why would you personally visit me to hand me a letter from my sister containing mere trivialities?"

t_ch: 「为了让你深入了解此事。你知道知更鸟如今正身在何方么？」
t_en: "To help you grasp the full scope of this issue. Do you know where Robin is at this moment?"

t_ch: 「依信中内容来看…应该是卡斯别林亚特-Ⅷ吧？她正在那里巡游……」
t_en: "From what the letter indicates... She must be in Kasbelina-VIII, correct? She's touring there right now..."

t_ch: 「不错。她可提到身中流弹一事？」
t_en: "Correct. Has she mentioned anything about a stray bullet?"

t_ch: 「流弹？什么……」
t_en: "A stray bullet? What..."

t_ch: 「那颗星球爆发了战争。正因如此，知更鸟才会将那里选作目的地…为了传扬『同谐』，挽救星球上的生命，她亲自奔赴前线了。」
t_en: "A war has broken out on that planet. It is because of this very reason that Robin chose this destination... To spread the word of the Harmony, and to save the lives of that planet, she personally made for the frontlines."

t_ch: 「她希望用歌声平复人们的痛苦，也愿意用自己的血肉之躯，为公司的救援物资输送提供援护…可惜，流弹无情。」
t_en: "She hoped to ease the people's suffering with song, and was willing to brave mortal danger to deliver the IPC's medical supplies... Unfortunately, stray bullets show no such compassion."

t_ch: 「她现在怎么样了？！」
t_en: "Is she alright!?"

t_ch: 「若手术成功，现在应该在野战医院里休养吧。」
t_en: "If the operation was successful, she should probably be recovering in the field hospital."

t_ch: 「星神在上，那枚子弹直接打进了她的脖子…不过，或许是平日践行『同谐』善举的回报，子弹没有伤及命脉。等你处理完琐事，尽快给她回信为好。」
t_en: "By the Aeon above, the bullet struck her neck directly... Yet, possibly as a reward for her consistent deeds of Harmony, it didn't hit any vital arteries. Once you've attended to your outstanding tasks, it'd be advisable to write her back as soon as possible."

t_ch: 「那群…该死的野蛮人！我现在就收拾行李…感谢您告诉我这些，歌斐木先生！」
t_en: "Those... damned savages! I'll pack my bags right away... My gratitude for bringing this to my attention, Mr. Gopher Wood!"

t_ch: 现在，你们知道她为何要时常佩戴那样繁琐的颈饰了吧？
t_en: Now, you understand why she always wears such elaborate neck ornaments, don't you?

t_ch: 怎么会这样…知更鸟小姐……
t_en: How could this happen... Miss Robin...

t_ch: 已经是过去的事了，各位不必在意。我分享此事，也只是希望你们理解「同谐」的局限和困境。「以强援弱」的愿景再伟大，多数时候也只是一厢情愿。
t_en: It's all in the past, so please don't worry. I share this in the meager hope that you will understand the Harmony's limitations and predicament. As grandiose as "the strong defending the weak" sounds, many times, it is nothing more than wishful thinking.

t_ch: 同样地，我为各位准备了最后一道课题，最后一次选择。但请放心，这次选择不会带来任何沉重的结果。
t_en: Likewise, I've prepared one last question, one last choice. But rest assured, this choice will not have any grave consequences.

t_ch: 事实上，什么事都不会发生。因为这只是一个空想，一道纠缠了我无数个夜晚的梦魇——如果各位有机会像我一样做出选择……
t_en: Because this is merely a figment of imagination, a nightmare that has haunted me through countless nights. If you ever had the opportunity to make a choice like I did...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>你们还会支持知更鸟踏上「同谐」的旅途么？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Would you still support Robin's journey on the Path of Harmony?</color>

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐竟然遭遇过这种事…「以强援弱」固然伟大，但如果要付出这种代价…我、我有点不知道了……
t_en: I can't believe that happened to Miss Robin... "The strong defending the weak" is a great mantra, but if I had to pay such a price... I, I don't know what I'd do...

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐的勇气令人敬佩，我本以为她只是在舞台上绽放光芒的明星……
t_en: Miss Robin's courage is admirable. And here I was, thinking she was just another superstar celebrity...

t_ch: 但她也是星期日先生的妹妹…即便拥有再伟大的理想，他一定也不希望至亲为此献身吧。
t_en: But the fact that she's also Mr. Sunday's younger sister... No, I doubt he'd wish harm on his own flesh and blood, no matter how grand the ambition.

t_ch: 总觉得，我在某些夜晚梦见过类似的场面。在梦中，我看见一群模糊的面孔，虽然不知道她们是谁，但我们情同家人。一直在与某种超然的存在抗争……
t_en: I often feel like I've dreamt of similar scenes on certain nights. In the dream, I see blurry faces. I don't know who they are, but I sympathize with all of them, fighting for survival against some unfathomable force...

t_ch: 我清楚地记得她们的迷茫、恐惧…但也记得，在那梦里她们从未选择放弃——就像知更鸟小姐一样。
t_en: Their confusion and fear are lucid to me... But I also remember they chose never to give up — just like Miss Robin.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，如果你对星期日先生的问题感到迷茫，就从自己的经历中寻找答案吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, if Mr. Sunday's question leaves you puzzled, you should find the answer from your own experiences.

t_ch: 每一次的「开拓」之旅都伴随着艰难险阻，但你真的会打退堂鼓吗？会阻止三月和丹恒前往下一站吗？我想你心中一定有属于自己的回答。
t_en: With each Trailblaze, dangers and tribulations will surely follow, but would you ever back away? Would you stop March and Dan Heng from reaching their next destination? I believe you have an answer of your own in your heart.

t_ch: 要做出这个选择吗？
t_en: Will you make this choice?

t_ch: 做出选择。
t_en: Make the decision.

t_ch: 再想想看。
t_en: Think it over some more.

t_ch: 呵…原来如此。
t_en: Heh... I see.

t_ch: 各位的主张，我已明了。
t_en: I am now aware of everyone's stances.

t_ch: 提出这些问题，只是为了阐明一件事：匹诺康尼的困境无法由「同谐」拯救，真正能建立起美梦乐园的——唯有以强制弱的「秩序」。
t_en: Raising these questions merely serves to illustrate one point: The plight of Penacony cannot be salvaged by the Harmony. The true foundation for a Sweetdream Paradise can only be established through the Order where the strong govern the weak.

t_ch: 我晓得人遭受折磨时如何痛苦，迷失道路时如何茫然，事与愿违时又如何沮丧…甚至绝望。这一切都令我痛苦，因为这样根本不能算是「幸福」。
t_en: I know the suffering of being tormented, the turmoil of losing your way, how sorrow... and even despair, set in when matters don't work out. All of this causes me unending pain, because this is not what "happiness" is at all.

t_ch: 我们必须教导弱者如何幸福地生活。而这「生活」并非名流贵族挂在嘴边的讲究，而是绝对意义上的，属于人的生存之道。
t_en: We must teach the weak how to live a happy life. And this life isn't some noble propriety that the upper crust preaches, but in definitive terms, a way of survival that belongs to everyone.

t_ch: 在你看来…怎样才算是幸福地活着？
t_en: So, what is your definition of living a happy life?

t_ch: 好问题。人类的意识本质上是种幻觉，是一座座名为「自我价值」的监牢。人被这幻觉诱导，犯下错误，后果却要由外物承担。
t_en: Good question. Human consciousness is fundamentally an illusion, a cage known as "self-worth." People lured in by this illusion, make mistakes, yet still ask that external influences bear the burden.

t_ch: 当一重又一重的错误充满人群，变得无从追溯…这一座座监牢便共同组成了一幢监狱，一条名为「适者生存」的自然法则。
t_en: When one mistake after the next permeates the masses, they become impossible to trace... Thus, the amassing of these individual cages culminate to form a prison, a place dictated only by the rule of "survival of the fittest."

t_ch: 而「自然」总是伴随着掠夺与牺牲…它的反面，叫做「秩序」。
t_en: Nature is always accompanied by predation and sacrifice... Its antithesis is known as Order.

t_ch: 我要做的正是这样的事：将众生的幸福归于唯一的「秩序」之下。人们不必再做出苦涩的抉择，不必再直面人性的弱点，抛却野兽的痼习，才能建立属于人的乐园。
t_en: That is what I want to do: Unite people's happiness under the banner of Order. They won't need to make bitter choices any longer, nor face the weaknesses of humanity. They can cast aside their primal instincts, to build a haven for mankind.

t_ch: 单单描述思想还是太过抽象，让我举个简单的例子吧。各位也许知道，在某些世界存在着名为「双休日」或「三休日」的社会运作制度。
t_en: Simply describing thoughts is far too abstract, so allow me to provide a simple example: As you all may know, there are societal norms like "weekends" and "long weekends" that exist on some worlds.

t_ch: 在来之不易的休息日里，人们得以从生活的重压中解脱，回归灵魂的平静。
t_en: During these hard-earned rest days, people are given the chance to extricate themselves from the stresses of everyday life, allowing a certain tranquility to return to their souls.

t_ch: 也只有在这样的日子里，人们不必面对弱肉强食的法则，能够在这短短数日中「幸福地活着」。只可惜…两三个日夜相较漫长的人生还是太过短暂。
t_en: And it is only on these days, that people do not have to adhere to the law where the strong prey on the weak — they can live out their lives happily during these brief intermissions. It's just a pity... that two or three days are still too fleeting compared to the span of a lifetime.

t_ch: 在我看来，社会的理想制度应当是「七休日」。在星期日的明天，是第二、第三、乃至永远的又一个星期日——这就是新世界的面貌，无所事事的永恒安宁之日。
t_en: From where I stand, society's ideal system should be "seven rest days." Following Sunday, there should ensue a second, a third, and indeed an infinite procession of Sundays. This should be the face of the new world — idyllic, eternal, peaceful days.

t_ch: 由此，每个人都能在乐园中回归自己原本的位置。有人瞻仰银河，全神贯注地计算孤绝世界{RUBY_B#银轨的起点}「裴伽纳」{RUBY_E#}离我们的距离，有人在角落彼此相拥，不必承担多余的职责……
t_en: And thus, every person can return to their base selves in this utopia. Some gaze in reverence at the stars, pouring their whole beings into calculating the distance between us and the isolated world of {RUBY_B#Silver Rail's starting point}Pegana{RUBY_E#}. Meanwhile, some seek refuge in quiet corners, holding one another, unencumbered by the chains of unwelcome obligations...

t_ch: 无需再承受现实之苦，唯有如此，人类才能以最高洁的姿态面对命中注定的结局，度过充满尊严的一生。这就是「幸福地活着」。
t_en: There would be no need to bear the hardships of reality. Only in this way can humanity face the inevitable end with the purest of spirit. Living a life of dignity — this is what it is to live in bliss.

t_ch: 流萤小姐——患有失熵症的你，一定能理解这意味着什么吧？
t_en: Miss Firefly, you who are stricken with Entropy Loss Syndrome — you of all would surely understand this?

t_ch: 听起来…好像无懈可击啊……
t_en: It sounds... like a flawless theory...

t_ch: 那…这一切的代价是什么？
t_en: But... what is the price to attain all this?

t_ch: 代价微不足道，只是一场属于我个人的…永久殉难。如果要为万众维持这座乐园，总得有一人陷入孤独的清醒中，直到宇宙的尽头。
t_en: The cost is minute, merely a personal... and eternal sacrifice. If this paradise is to be maintained for everyone, someone must remain trapped in solitary wakening, until the end of the cosmos.

t_ch: 清醒…也就是说，那乐园仍是一场梦。踏入乐园，便意味着要放弃现实，对么？
t_en: Wakening... Which means that this so-called paradise is still a dream. Stepping into this paradise means forsaking reality, correct?

t_ch: 这并非放弃，而是超越。血肉苦弱。如果物质是精神苦难的根源，那我们理应战胜它。
t_en: It is not forsaking, but transcending. Flesh, blood, sorrow, weakness. If the physical is the root of spiritual suffering, it is only logical that we defeat it.

t_ch: 但在这样的「幸福」中，人们从未战胜苦难，也永远失去了战胜苦难的机会。换一种说法…这是「逃避」。
t_en: But in this supposed "bliss," people won't have defeated their demons. The chance to overcome their tribulations would be forever lost to them. In other words... it is an escape.

t_ch: 你可以这么认为，但逃避并不可耻。恰恰相反，每个人心中都有逃避的种子。
t_en: That's another way of understanding it, but there is no shame in escape. On the contrary, the seeds of escape exist in everyone's hearts.

t_ch: 流萤小姐不也这么觉得吗，生命因何而沉睡？是因为人们害怕从梦中醒来。
t_en: Don't you agree, Miss Firefly? And as for why life slumbers? It is because we are afraid to awaken from our dreams.

t_ch: 但这与伟大的事业并不冲突。唯有承认这点，我们才能理解人性的软弱，进而包容，进而庇佑。
t_en: But this is not in conflict with the grand plan. Only in acknowledging this, can we truly understand the frailty of human nature, and from there, show compassion and protection.

t_ch: 我…认可你是一位天生的领导者。你对人类充满悲观，却依然怀抱着否定的心，予以众生平等的怜悯。
t_en: I... I admit that you are a born leader. Your perspective on humanity brims with pessimism, yet you express compassion for all, even when your heart pities them.

t_ch: 但我和你不同，我是为「自我」而活的。在我看来，人为自己做出选择，是理所应当的行为，也是他们与生俱来的权利。
t_en: But unlike you — I live for the "self." From my perspective, individuals making choices for themselves is their birthright.

t_ch: 也许逃避是弱者的天性，但谁是弱者…不应由他人来定义。
t_en: The want to escape may be innate in the weak, but whether they are weak or not... It is not up to another to decide.

t_ch: 难道在你眼中，我也要被归类为弱者吗？
t_en: Perhaps in your mind, you also view me as "weak"?

t_ch: 我并不这么觉得。
t_en: Because I don't think so.

t_ch: 既然流萤小姐已经表明了自己的观点，星穹列车自然也会给出我们的回答。
t_en: Since Miss Firefly has said her piece, the Astral Express will also naturally give you our answer.

t_ch: 交给你了，{NICKNAME}，就像米哈伊尔先生嘱咐的那样，告诉他我们的选择吧——
t_en: We'll leave it to you, {NICKNAME}. Just as Mr. Mikhail instructed before: Tell him our choice...

t_ch: 两位，请接受「纯美」的考验！
t_en: Dear friends, please ready yourselves for this trial set forth by the Beauty!

t_ch: 各位的信念令人惊叹……
t_en: Your convictions, my friends, are indeed awe-inspiring...

t_ch: 但很可惜…桂冠依旧属于伊德莉拉！
t_en: It's a pity... The laurels of victory belong to Idrila!

t_ch: 够了！我已明白各位的真意…我服输！
t_en: That's enough! I have come to grasp the sincerity of your cause... I yield!

t_ch: 不久前……
t_en: Not long ago...

t_ch: 请问，这里是……
t_en: What is this place...?

t_ch: 你对这里有印象吗，米沙？
t_en: Does this place ring any bells, Misha?

t_ch: 我…说不上来。但好像有种似曾相识的感觉。这是哪儿？
t_en: I... don't know, but I feel a sense of déjà vu. What is this place?

t_ch: 这是一枚梦泡，我们的意识进入了其中。这是一位无名客留给星穹列车的礼物，但奇怪的是，我们打开时却发现里面什么都没有。
t_en: It's the realm within a dream bubble. This was left to the Astral Express by a Nameless, but weirdly when we entered it, it was completely empty.

t_ch: 梦境贩售店的爱德华医生告诉我，梦境由记忆凝聚而成，如果内核空无一物，梦泡是无法成形的。
t_en: Dr. Edward from the Dreamscape Sales Store told me that dreams are formed from memories, and a dream bubble can't take shape if its core is empty.

t_ch: 所以我想，作为酒店门童，星穹列车的朋友中最了解匹诺康尼的人，小米沙，你应该能帮我们解开这个疑惑？
t_en: So I thought you might be able to help us in unraveling this mystery, Misha? As a hotel {RUBY_B#bellboy}doorman{RUBY_E#}, you know Penacony best among us.

t_ch: 唔，梦泡的事我懂的不多。但如果各位想弄明白这幢大房子是什么…我会努力试试的！
t_en: Hmm, I don't know much about dream bubbles, but if you want to figure out what this mansion is... I'll do my best!

t_ch: 那就拜托你了。
t_en: I'm counting on you then.

t_ch: 姬子姐…我还是很疑惑，为什么你就断定<color=#dbc291ff>米沙和这枚梦泡有关</color>呢？
t_en: Himeko... I still don't get it. Why were you so sure that <color=#dbc291ff>Misha had a connection with this dream bubble</color>?

t_ch: 我无法断定，只是有些猜测。但既然米沙对这里感到熟悉…说明我的猜测可能是正确的。
t_en: I wasn't sure. It was just a hunch, but since Misha feels familiar with this place... my hunch might be correct.

t_ch: 这个场景有点眼熟…
t_en: This place looks a bit familiar...

t_ch: 这个场景有点怀念…
t_en: This place feels a bit nostalgic...

t_ch: 没错，你和流萤小姐就是在这里遭遇了「死亡」——现在该叫它「沉眠」了。考虑到它和流梦礁的关联，出现在这倒是不难理解。
t_en: Exactly, this is where you and Firefly encountered "Death," which we now know as "Dormancy." Considering its connection to Dreamflux Reef, it's not surprising it appeared here.

t_ch: 现在的问题是，是谁把你们带进了这里。依照目前的线索来看，可能并不是那位愚者，那么他是谁就十分关键了。
t_en: The question now is — Who brought you here? Based on the clues we have so far, it's unlikely to be that Masked Fool, so identifying them is crucial to us.

t_ch: 我们已经再次接近真相的核心。给米沙一点时间吧，他一定能为我们揭开梦泡的秘密。
t_en: We're drawing closer to the truth once more. Let's give Misha some time, as I believe he'll unveil the secret of this dream bubble.

t_ch: 好吧。可这里到处都是门，该从哪扇门出去呢…米沙，你知道吗？
t_en: Alright, but there are doors all over the place... Which one should we choose...? Do you have any idea, Misha?

t_ch: 唔，我想…可能是这边？
t_en: Hmm... I guess... maybe this way?

t_ch: 我不确定，但…试试看吧。
t_en: I... I'm not entirely sure, but... let's give it a try.

t_ch: 竟然一下子就选对了？
t_en: Wait, you managed to choose the right door on your first try?

t_ch: 这么说来，钟表小子去哪了…是去守护美梦小镇了吗？
t_en: So, where has Clockie gone...? Did he leave to protect Dreamville?

t_ch: 我好像听见了水声……
t_en: I think I hear the sound of water...

t_ch: 记得{NICKNAME}说过，前面有个特别壮观的喷泉。
t_en: {NICKNAME} once mentioned there's a magnificent fountain up ahead.

t_ch: 看，就是这儿。
t_en: Look, there it is.

t_ch: 这里是要去对面吧。
t_en: We're going to the opposite side, right?

t_ch: 不，这里应该走左边……
t_en: No, we should turn left here...

t_ch: 咦，感觉路不太一样了？
t_en: Huh? Something feels different about this place?

t_ch: 就是这，我记得这条走廊。
t_en: This is it. I remember this corridor.

t_ch: 前面是…米哈伊尔的书房。最后一次见爷爷，就是在那边。
t_en: Up ahead is... Grandpa's study. It was in that room that I saw him the last time.

t_ch: 就是这儿…！
t_en: This is it...!

t_ch: 奇怪，这里明明不是酒店，差别也很大，但我总觉得自己来过，而且…住过一段时间。
t_en: Weird, this place is quite different from the hotel, but I just... I feel like I've been here before and... even lived here for a while.

t_ch: 如果没记错，沿着那条走廊向前，有一座温暖的壁炉。我和钟表小子常常在那烤火，听木柴噼啪作响。
t_en: If I remember correctly, there should be a fireplace down that hallway. Clockie and I used to sit by the fire, listening to the crackling of firewood.

t_ch: 而另一边的房间是…是放玩具的屋子。我喜欢把箱子里的东西一股脑儿在地上摊开，然后挨个给他们编故事。
t_en: And... And the room on the other side was... the toy room. I loved spreading out all the toys from the box on the floor and making up stories for each of them.

t_ch: 不对，不对，我不是在流梦礁长大的吗？那这里又是……
t_en: Hold on, this doesn't make sense. Didn't I grow up in Dreamflux Reef? So, what is this place...?

t_ch: 这可能是一种名为「失忆」的现象。别担心，小米沙，每个人总会忘记一些过去。但它们并没有消失，只是沉睡在了脑海深处。同样地，我们也能把它们找回来。
t_en: This could be a case of amnesia. But don't worry, Misha, it's common for everyone to forget certain aspects of the past. Those memories haven't vanished. They're just lying in the depths of your mind. We can surely get them back.

t_ch: 既然你对这里有印象，我们就再去几个房间，看看能不能想起更多，好不好？
t_en: Since this place seems familiar to you, why don't we explore a few more rooms and see if you can recall anything more?

t_ch: 好。那，就去我刚才说的两个房间看看吧。
t_en: Yeah, then let's check out the rooms I just mentioned.

t_ch: 房间里有朦胧的声音…折纸小鸟？是好朋友的名字。
t_en: I heard some noises from the room... Origami Bird? That's a friend of mine.

t_ch: 你和折纸小鸟也是朋友吗？
t_en: You and Origami Bird are friends?

t_ch: 嗯，它和钟表小子、镜子小姐一样，都是「罗盘号」的船员。折纸小鸟不止一只！它们是个大家庭，有许多长得一模一样的兄弟姐妹。
t_en: Yeah, it's a member of the Compass crew, just like Clockie and Miss Mirror. And there's more than just one Origami Bird! They are a big family with lots of brothers and sisters who look the same.

t_ch: 它们听从镜子小姐的安排，在船上负责各种工作，是最好的水手。
t_en: They follow Miss Mirror's orders and handle all sorts of jobs on the ship. They're the best sailors.

t_ch: 水手？折纸小鸟有这种设定吗……
t_en: Sailors? Can Origami Birds be sailors...?

t_ch: 小米沙，能再讲讲那艘「罗盘号」的故事吗？
t_en: Could you tell us more about the "Compass," Misha?

t_ch: 「罗盘号」是开往新大陆的船！它带领钟表小子和伙伴们穿越层层迷雾，航向大海深处。每次遇到危险，钟表小子就会操作罗盘，把大家带往正确的方向！
t_en: The "Compass" is a ship bound for the New World! Clockie and his partners travel through layers of fog to the depths of the sea. Whenever there is danger, Clockie will use a compass and guide the ship in the right direction!

t_ch: 真是个好故事。不过在匹诺康尼的动画里，钟表小子和伙伴们一直都生活在美梦小镇，似乎从来没有离开过？
t_en: That's a great story, but in the Penacony cartoon, Clockie and his partners have always lived in Dreamville and never ventured out, right?

t_ch: 咦，好像确实是这样。
t_en: Huh? Oh... that does seem to be the case.

t_ch: 奇怪，可我明明记得…钟表小子最后抵达了新大陆……
t_en: Weird... I clearly remember... Clockie arrived in the New World in the end...

t_ch: 也许钟表小子还有我们不知道的过去呢。
t_en: Perhaps Clockie has a hidden past.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔——就是这个名字，现在我们都知道他是「钟表匠」了。
t_en: Mikhail, that's the name. Now we all know him as the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 所以，他是在和谁说话？小米沙，你知道什么吗？
t_en: So, who is he talking to? Do you know anything about it, Misha?

t_ch: 抱歉，我不太清楚「钟表匠」的事。但「米哈伊尔」……
t_en: I'm sorry, I don't know much about the Watchmaker, but Mikhail...

t_ch: 想起什么了吗？
t_en: Anything special about that name?

t_ch: 「米哈伊尔」…是爷爷的名字。
t_en: Mikhail... is Grandpa's name.

t_ch: 爷爷？什么意思，莫非你是「钟表匠」的后代？
t_en: Grandpa? Do you mean... you're the Watchmaker's grandson?

t_ch: 可我们先前了解到的故事里，并没提到「钟表匠」有后人。这个名字也不少见，可能只是个巧合。
t_en: But we haven't heard anything about the Watchmaker having descendants, and the name "Mikhail" is not rare. Perhaps it's merely a coincidence.

t_ch: 小米沙，能和我们详细介绍一下那位米哈伊尔爷爷吗？
t_en: Could you tell us more about your grandpa Mikhail?

t_ch: 嗯，当然。米哈伊尔是位航海士，再神秘的海域，再凶险的风暴都难不倒他。他总是出门在外，有许多朋友和他一起旅行。
t_en: Yeah, sure. He was a seafarer who fearlessly ventured into mysterious seas and storms. He was always on the sea and had a lot of friends who accompanied him on his travels.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔不喜欢别人叫他爷爷，他说听着显老，自己还年轻。「米哈伊尔」是父母留给他的名字——{RUBY_B#米哈-伊尔}米哈利和伊丽丝{RUBY_E#}，两位伟大的海上旅行者。
t_en: He didn't want me to call him "Grandpa" because that would make him sound old. He believed he was still young. The name "Mikhail" was given to him by his parents, Mihaly and Elise, both renowned seafarers.

t_ch: 每次回来，他都会给我看航海日志，说起海上发生的事。我的梦想就是成为和米哈伊尔一样的冒险家。
t_en: Every time he came back, he'd share his logbook with me and tell me about his adventures at sea. I want to become a great adventurer just like him.

t_ch: 听起来确实和「钟表匠」没什么关系啊，真的只是同名同姓？
t_en: It appears that the seafarer has nothing to do with the Watchmaker. So, perhaps it's just a coincidence?

t_ch: 所以那位爷爷，现在在哪呢？
t_en: So, where is your grandpa now?

t_ch: 他踏上新的旅程了，我们已经好久没见啦。
t_en: He went off on a new journey, and it's been quite a while since I last saw him.

t_ch: 「池水像块宝石，镶嵌在所有扬帆之人的梦中。」
t_en: "The water resembles a precious jewel, embedded in the dreams of all seafarers."

t_ch: 「每个漂泊的日子，凝视波涛下的光点，我仿佛又回到这里，回到你们的身边……」
t_en: "Every time I gaze at the shimmering lights beneath the waves, it feels as though I'm back in this place, standing by your side..."

t_ch: 米沙，又想起什么了吗？
t_en: Have you recalled anything, Misha?

t_ch: 这是米哈伊尔写在航海日志里的话。爷爷说，虽然出海要面临许多危险，但每当他站在午后的甲板上，望向海面上的粼粼波光时，他总会想起家门口这座喷泉。
t_en: Yeah, I saw these sentences in Grandpa's logbook. He used to say that despite the perils of the sea, whenever he stood on the deck in the afternoon, overlooking the sparkling waves, he would think of this fountain in front of his house.

t_ch: 他常说，那一刻就像回到了家人身边，海上的日子再苦，也不觉得多难熬了。
t_en: He often said that those moments felt like returning to his family's side, and the difficulties at sea didn't seem quite as challenging.

t_ch: 唉，这种心情，我也不是不能体会……
t_en: *sigh* You know, I quite understand such sentiments...

t_ch: 别叹气，要有白头发了…
t_en: Don't sigh, or you'll start getting gray hair...

t_ch: 在、在哪？别闹！我就是有感而发嘛。
t_en: Hey, don't tease! I was just being a bit sentimental.

t_ch: 别倚老卖老啊。
t_en: You're talking like an elder.

t_ch: 我可没有倚老卖老，就是有感而发嘛。
t_en: I'm not an elder. I was just being a bit sentimental.

t_ch: 也许每个远离家乡的冒险者心中都会有这么一座喷泉。纵使大海的那一头充满未知，家门口的喷泉却是爷爷的罗盘，总能引领他回到思念的亲人身边。
t_en: Perhaps every adventurer far from home carries a fountain within their soul. Even though the other side of the sea remains shrouded in the unknown, the fountain in front of his house serves as a compass, leading him back to his cherished ones.

t_ch: 是啊，米哈伊尔在家的时候，我们就会站在喷泉边，在池子里放下「罗盘号」——我自己做的玩具船！
t_en: Yeah, while Grandpa was at home, we would stand by the fountain and place the "Compass" — a toy boat that I made — into the pool!

t_ch: 那时我会问他，自己什么时候可以像他一样踏上冒险。米哈伊尔总是笑着说，小孩子还太年轻。
t_en: Back then, I would ask him when I could go on adventures like him, and he would always laugh and say I was still too young.

t_ch: 这么看来，这位「米哈伊尔」还真是一位航海士，和咱们所知的「钟表匠」没什么关系。
t_en: It seems this "Mikhail" is truly a seafarer and has nothing to do with the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 嗯，从米沙的描述来看，梦泡里的场景也都是他童年时的回忆。
t_en: Yeah, based on Misha's recollections, the scenes in the dream bubble appear to be his childhood memories.

t_ch: 可这样一来谜团就更多了。根据小米沙的说法，他明显是个出生在海洋星球，过着平凡日子的小孩，和匹诺康尼没有半点关系……
t_en: But this raises more questions. According to Misha, he was clearly born on an oceanic planet and led an ordinary life, with no connection to Penacony at all...

t_ch: 难道这是什么比喻？「大海」指的是忆域？
t_en: Could this be some sort of metaphor? Perhaps the "sea" refers to the Memory Zone?

t_ch: 对不起，我也不知道。但记忆就像喷泉里的水一样不受控制地涌上来……
t_en: I'm sorry. I don't know, but my memories keep pouring out uncontrollably, like water flowing from a fountain...

t_ch: 也许…也许再往前走，我还能想起更多……
t_en: Perhaps... I'll remember more things if we go further...

t_ch: 就是这儿。这里…是我的<color=#dbc291ff>「钟表房」</color>。
t_en: This is it. This... is my <color=#dbc291ff>room of clocks</color>.

t_ch: 等待米哈伊尔航海归来的时间里，瓦尔德大叔给了我这间工作室，玩具间，我的「秘密据点」……
t_en: While I spent my time waiting for Grandpa to return from his voyage, Walder gave me this workshop, and it became my "secret base"...

t_ch: 我在这里学习修理发条和齿轮，我喜欢精密的机械，我是「罗盘号」的船长，和我的伙伴，钟表小子和镜子小姐一起，在梦中寻找新大陆。
t_en: Here, I learned how to repair clockwork and gears out of my fondness of precision mechanics. In my dreams, I was the captain of the "Compass," embarking on adventures with my companions, Clockie and Miss Mirror, in search of the New World.

t_ch: 我…是在这里诞生长大的。
t_en: I... I was born and raised here.

t_ch: 所以，梦泡中的这栋建筑是你童年的「家」。
t_en: So, this building in the dream bubble is your childhood "home."

t_ch: 是，但也不是。也许这么说更合适……
t_en: Yes, but not exactly. To be more precise...

t_ch: 这个「梦泡」，就是我的家。
t_en: This "dream bubble" itself is my home.

t_ch: 看来，你已经全部想起来了。
t_en: Looks like you've remembered everything now.

t_ch: 等等等等，这种除我以外的人全部心有灵犀的感觉是怎么回事？
t_en: Wait, wait! Why does it feel like everyone else knows something I don't?

t_ch: 没错，就你还被蒙在鼓里啦。
t_en: Yeah, you're the only one still in the dark.

t_ch: 其实我也不知道…
t_en: Too bad I don't know either...

t_ch: 小三月，你还记得{NICKNAME}先前提到过，有个只有{M#他}{F#她}才能看见的钟表小子么？
t_en: March, do you remember when {NICKNAME} mentioned a "Clockie" that only {M#he}{F#she} could see?

t_ch: 记得呀，不就是边上这位？可是在流梦礁，咱们不是都见着它了，杨叔也和它打过招呼呢。
t_en: Yeah, the little guy here, right? But we all saw him in Dreamflux Reef, right? And Mr. Yang even greeted him.

t_ch: 看来星穹列车的各位都非常具有童心呢！
t_en: Looks like everyone on the Astral Express has a childlike spirit!

t_ch: 答案正是「星穹列车」。{NICKNAME}的经历证明，至少流萤小姐和黄泉小姐是看不见这位钟表小子的。
t_en: The answer lies in the Astral Express. {NICKNAME}'s experience shows that neither Firefly nor Acheron can see this Clockie.

t_ch: 而在流梦礁，不知各位是否有注意到，列车组以外的其他人…都微妙地没有和它发生过任何对话。
t_en: And when we were in Dreamflux Reef, you may have noticed that for some reason, nobody outside of the Crew... had ever talked with Clockie.

t_ch: 我也发现了。
t_en: Yeah, I noticed that, too.

t_ch: 还有这种事？
t_en: Wait, really?

t_ch: 只有特定人群才能看见的模因生命，简直就像是…某个人留给无名客的密信一样。
t_en: A memetic life that can only be seen by a select few... It's just like a hidden message... left by someone for the Nameless.

t_ch: 可这么说，小米沙不是也能看见钟表小子？他们甚至是从小一起长大的好伙伴，可米沙还没有踏上「开拓」之道啊……
t_en: But Misha can see Clockie, too, right? They even grew up together, but Misha hasn't started the way of the Trailblaze yet...

t_ch: 这就是谜底的关键所在， 小三月。现在，再试着回忆一下……
t_en: That's the key to the mystery, March. Now, take a moment to recall...

t_ch: 就像钟表小子一样，你见过任何列车组以外的人，和米沙产生过互动吗？
t_en: Have you ever seen anyone outside of the Crew interact with Misha?

t_ch: 不会吧……
t_en: Wait... no way...

t_ch: 答案就是如此，三月七小姐。这枚梦泡是我诞生的摇篮，我…是一位和忆域迷因无异的梦中人。
t_en: That's the answer, March 7th. This dream bubble is the place where I was born, and I... I'm a dweller in this dream just like a Memory Zone Meme.

t_ch: 我本应留在梦泡中等待各位到访，但现实和记忆重叠在一起，让我不自觉地推门而出，带着钟表小子离开了这里。
t_en: I should have stayed here and waited for you, but when reality and memories merged, I unconsciously pushed open that door and left the bubble with Clockie.

t_ch: 所以「钟表匠」留下的梦泡空无一物，不是因为没有内容，而是因为…其中的内容擅自离开了？
t_en: So, it's not that the Watchmaker's dream bubble is empty, but rather... the stuff inside ran away?

t_ch: 你听到的鸣笛声，就是列车抵达匹诺康尼的声音？
t_en: And the whistle you heard was the sound of the Express arriving at Penacony?

t_ch: 这的确是一种解读。但它背后应该还有一段更为漫长的故事。我想，一切的来龙去脉，迷惑难解之处，还是由他本人来解释吧……
t_en: That's one way to see it, but I believe there is a longer story behind all this. It's best for Misha himself to explain all the details...

t_ch: 不如就从<color=#dbc291ff>你的名字</color>开始吧？现在，我们应该怎么称呼你，「米沙」，还是……
t_en: How about we start with <color=#dbc291ff>your name</color>? Now, should we call you Misha? Or...

t_ch: 感谢各位帮我找回这段漫长的旅程，容我重新做个自我介绍吧。
t_en: Thank you all for helping me rediscover my true self. Now, please allow me to reintroduce myself.

t_ch: 我出生在普热斯米尔星系的露莎卡星，是航海家米哈伊尔先生和夏尔太太的养子。两位老人给了我一件宝物，一个承载了他们希望寄语的名字——
t_en: I was born on Lushaka in the Przesmir system, adopted by seafarers Mikhail and Char. They gave me a treasure, a name that carried their hopes...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔

t_ch: 我们到流梦礁了。然后，要去哪里？
t_en: We've arrived at Dreamflux Reef. So, where to next?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 钟表小子，我应该…不会再去哪里了。
t_en: You know, Clockie. I don't think... I'll be going anywhere else.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 我已经走得够远了。是时候，稍微休息下了……
t_en: I've traveled far enough, and it's time for a little break...

t_ch: 哦，那等你休息好了，我们再出发？
t_en: Oh, so we'll set out again when you're rested?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 不，我应该会留在这里。然后…就结束了。
t_en: No, I'll stay here, and then... this is where it ends.

t_ch: 结束？米沙，这是什么意思？
t_en: This is where it ends? What do you mean, Misha?

t_ch: 你明明说过，「开拓」之旅永远也不会结束。
t_en: You told me that the Trailblazing expedition would never end.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 是啊，我是这么说过……
t_en: Yeah, that's what I said...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 所以现在，该你决定自己的下一站了。
t_en: So now, it's up to you to decide your next destination.

t_ch: 我的下一站？那应该是哪里？我从来都是跟着你的……
t_en: My next destination? What's that supposed to be? I've been following you...

t_ch: 米沙？你怎么了，今天的你好奇怪！如果不开心，我们可以像平时那样——施展「钟表把戏」！
t_en: Misha? You're acting weird today! If you're feeling down, we can just do what we usually do... with the clockwork!

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 不用了，我没有不开心。至于「钟表把戏」…是啊，在这片梦里它仿佛能解决一切问题。
t_en: No, I'm not feeling down. As for clockwork... yeah, it resolves all problems in this dream.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 那么你知道，「钟表把戏」究竟是什么吗？
t_en: So, do you know what clockwork actually is?

t_ch: 是什么？我不知道。
t_en: Hmm? I'm not quite sure.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 每个人都会有迷路的时候，犹豫不决，不知道该去往哪个方向。它存在于这片梦境中，也存在于梦境之外的任何地方。
t_en: Well, everyone gets lost at times. They may hesitate and doubt which way to go. That happens in this Dreamscape and beyond.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 但无需害怕，正如人们会感到迷茫，在某个瞬间，他们也会下定决心，做出一个大胆但又了不起的决定……
t_en: But don't worry, everyone goes through moments of uncertainty and hesitation. Eventually, they gather the courage to make bold decisions...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 无论那是镇静的、欢欣的、愤怒的、还是悲伤的，他们需要的只是一道小小的推力，然后就能迈开步伐，走向属于自己的前方。
t_en: Whether it's calming, happy, angry, or sad, all they need is a little nudge to take that step toward where they truly belong.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 我把这小小的力量留给你，并期待你将它带给更多的人……
t_en: I'm leaving that little nudge with you, and I hope you'll share it with others...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 这就是「钟表把戏」，名为「开拓」的意志。
t_en: Such is the essence of clockwork, the will of the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 好了，各位。让我们来作出决定吧。
t_en: All right, everyone. Let's make a decision.

t_ch: 但我想，应该不会有人有异议吧。
t_en: Although I don't think anyone will have any objections.

t_ch: 我会选择「开拓」。
t_en: I'll choose the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 当然，都走到这一步了，还有「前进」以外的选项吗？
t_en: Of course. We've come this far. Surely there's no other option than moving forward.

t_ch: 既然这样，就是全票通过了。
t_en: In that case, it's unanimous.

t_ch: 我想要见证遗产。
t_en: I want to witness the legacy.

t_ch: 当然，都走到这一步了，还有「前进」以外的选项吗？
t_en: Of course. We've come this far. Surely there's no other option than moving forward.

t_ch: 既然这样，就是全票通过了。
t_en: In that case, it's unanimous.

t_ch: 我有异议！
t_en: I object!

t_ch: 你啊…这种时候就别闹腾了，都走到这一步了，还有「前进」以外的选项吗？
t_en: Come on... Stop joking at a moment like this. We've come this far. Surely there's no other option than moving forward.

t_ch: 既然这样，投票结果就是二比一。
t_en: In that case, the vote stands at two to one.

t_ch: 那就让我们一起前往这场梦的终点…告诉米哈伊尔，我们的选择吧。
t_en: Then let's proceed together to the end of this dream... and tell Mikhail our decision.

t_ch: 这是「筑梦拼图」，对吧？是要我们把出口变出来？可是现在该上哪找这最后一块拼图呢……
t_en: This is the Dream Jigsaw, right? So, we're supposed to find the exit? But where can we find the last piece...?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你还记得吗？误入这里的时候，你有拿到过一块神秘的碎片。
t_en: Do you remember, {NICKNAME}? You said you obtained a mysterious shard when you stumbled into this place.

t_ch: （查看背包。）
t_en: (Check the inventory.)

t_ch: 哇，形状好像对得上，原来这块碎片也和小米沙有关？
t_en: Hey, the shape seems to match. So, this shard is also connected to Misha?

t_ch: 看来真相已经呼之欲出了。我们这就去出口的对面一探究竟吧。
t_en: Looks like we're just one step from revealing the truth. Let's get to the other side and investigate.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 米哈伊尔！你要去哪里……
t_en: Mikhail! Where are you going...?

t_ch: 总得有人站出来拯救露莎卡，小米沙，为什么不能是我？
t_en: Someone has to step up and save Lushaka. So, why can't it be me, Misha?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 不要走，好不好？或者带上我一起，求你了，不要离开……
t_en: Please, don't go. And if you must, please take me with you... Don't leave me alone...

t_ch: 就算没有我，你也知道该如何向前了。勇敢的米沙船长，「罗盘号」在等着你呢，你不是一直都想成为比我更厉害的冒险家吗？
t_en: Even without me, you know how to proceed forward, brave Captain Misha. The "Compass" is waiting for you. Haven't you always wanted to be a better adventurer than me?

t_ch: 走吧，登上那辆列车，然后…就开始你的旅途吧。
t_en: Now go. Board that train and... start your journey.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔，你要去哪里？
t_en: Where are you going, Mikhail?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 我、我要去观景车厢擦地了！我答应了列车长……
t_en: I... I'm going to clean the floor in the Parlor Car! I've promised the conductor...

t_ch: 站住。先告诉我，这表是你修好的？
t_en: Wait, first tell me, did you fix this watch?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 呃…是、是的。
t_en: Um... yeah.

t_ch: 我知道它原本长什么样，挂链断裂，背壳破损，刻度也都快磨没了…你是怎么做到的。
t_en: I know what it looked like before. Its chain was broken, the back case torn, and the marks on the dial all worn out... How did you manage to fix it?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 怎么做到的…我很难讲明白，但我知道它能被修好……
t_en: Well... It's hard to explain, but I knew it could be fixed...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 是指针，阿蒙森先生，它的指针还是好的，依旧能指向正确的方向，所以剩下的都有办法解决。
t_en: It's the hands, Mr. Amundsen. Its hands were intact and pointing in the right direction, so I knew there would be a way to fix the rest.

t_ch: 以后，你跟着我一起干。列车长那边我来搞定，你不是一直都想鼓捣列车吗？从今天起，你就是车上的机修工了。
t_en: I see, you'll work with me from now on. Haven't you always wanted to tinker with this train? You're its mechanic now. As for the conductor, I'll do the talking.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 可、可我只懂得修表……
t_en: B—But I only know how to fix watches...

t_ch: 别担心，一通百通，哪里缺了补哪里，我教你。
t_en: Don't worry, you've got what it takes. I'll teach you what you need to know.

t_ch: 拉格沃克，你要去哪里？我们该出发去下一站了。
t_en: Where are you going, Legwork? It's time to head to our next stop.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 我…可能不走了。我准备留在阿斯德纳，和拉扎莉娜跟铁尔南一起。
t_en: I... I'm staying in Asdana, with Razalina and Tiernan.

t_ch: 哦…这里让你想起自己的家了？
t_en: I see... This place reminds you of home?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 阿斯德纳人只是获得了一场小小的胜利，离真正的自由…还有很长的路要走。哈努努需要我们。
t_en: The people of Asdana have only achieved a tiny victory and still have a long way to go... towards true freedom. Hanunue needs us.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 放心，不是所有旅途都要通向星辰大海。就算离开了列车，我们的「开拓」也不会结束。
t_en: Don't worry, not all journeys lead to the stars. Even if I leave the Express, our Path of Trailblaze will continue.

t_ch: 没关系，我早知道你们几个是留不住的。安心去吧，朋友，把这个也带上。
t_en: Yeah, I knew you wouldn't stay on the Express forever. Leave in peace, my friend, and take this with you.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 这是…阿蒙森先生的帽子？为什么……
t_en: This is... Mr. Amundsen's hat? But why...?

t_ch: 他临走的时候，说要把它留给他最好的学生。我想，现在是时候了。
t_en: When he departed, he said he would leave it to his best student. Well, I suppose the time has come.

t_ch: 再见了，拉格沃克，照顾好铁尔南和拉扎莉娜。记得…要给我们写信啊。
t_en: Farewell, Legwork. Take care of Tiernan and Razalina, and don't forget to... write to us.

t_ch: 「钟表匠」，你要去哪里？
t_en: Where are you going, Watchmaker?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 放心吧，米凯。就是出趟远门而已。
t_en: Don't worry, Micah. Just going on a little trip.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 总得有人奔赴星际拓荒前线，现在匹诺康尼就剩一个「前无名客」了，为什么不能是我？
t_en: Someone has to be at the forefront of the interstellar frontier, and I'm the only former Nameless in Penacony. So, why can't it be me?

t_ch: 就是因为我们只剩下你了！你忘记铁尔南了吗？银河不像当初，太危险了！如果再失去你，匹诺康尼该怎么办？
t_en: Because you're all we have! Have you forgotten about Tiernan? The Cosmos is way more dangerous now! What will happen to Penacony if we lose you, too?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 如果我们找不到出路，匹诺康尼又该怎么办？
t_en: But what will happen to Penacony if we don't find a way out?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 铁尔南…我怎么可能忘记他，每一个不眠的夜晚我都在问自己，为什么当时没有和他一起出发？
t_en: Tiernan... How could I ever forget him? I've spent countless sleepless nights asking myself why I didn't go with him back then.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 无名客的脚步是停不下来的…安心吧，米凯。重操旧业而已，放心等我回来吧。但如果，我是说如果……
t_en: We Nameless won't stop... Don't worry, Micah, it's just a matter of getting back to my old profession. Just wait for me to come back. But if, and it's a big if...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 如果我没能全身而退，那就由你来当下一任「钟表匠」吧。
t_en: If I don't come back in one piece, then you'll become the next Watchmaker.

t_ch: 老头，你要去哪里？
t_en: Where are you going, old man?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 哦，原来你在啊？
t_en: Oh, you're here.

t_ch: 回答我的问题，你想干嘛？
t_en: Answer my question. What are you up to?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 别紧张，加拉赫，我只是想到了一个绝妙的点子，要听听么？
t_en: Relax, Gallagher. I just came up with a great idea. Wanna hear it?

t_ch: 得了吧！你哪个点子不是在把自己往火坑里推？老头，别怪我说话难听，匹诺康尼当年的英雄人物，现在可是只剩你一个了。
t_en: Oh, come on! Aren't all your "ideas" just ways to get yourself killed? I may be blunt here, but you're the last remaining hero in Penacony.

t_ch: 你要是死了，那星核的秘密…就再也没有重现天日的时候了。
t_en: If you die too, the secret of the Stellaron... will go to the grave with you.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 是啊，在匹诺康尼，恐怕咱们已经无路可走了。所以也只能把目光看向阿斯德纳之外了。
t_en: Yes, I'm afraid there's no way out in Penacony, so I'll have to consider alternatives beyond Asdana.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 我们要举办一场盛会，理由就用「『钟表匠』的遗产」吧。然后…向全银河发出邀请，把人们都聚集到这儿来。
t_en: We'll organize a festival, using the "Watchmaker's Legacy" as a facade... and send invitations to the entire Cosmos to gather people here.

t_ch: 你这是打算和家族破罐子破摔？
t_en: So? A desperate struggle against The Family?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 这不是还有你么，我的朋友？哈哈，这事是很困难，但咱们这一路走来，又有哪件事不难？哦对了，不管你用什么方法，记得……
t_en: Desperate? Hah, don't we have you here, my friend? This task is challenging, but what hasn't been challenging for us along the way? Well, whatever you do, remember...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 一定要把邀请函寄到星穹列车手中。
t_en: Make sure to send an invitation to the Astral Express.

t_ch: 米沙！你要去哪里？
t_en: Misha! Where are you going?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 嗯，钟表小子，带我去流梦礁吧……
t_en: Oh, it's you, Clockie... Take me to Dreamflux Reef...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 昨天晚上，我做了个很长的梦，梦见我们相遇的那天。我想把那个梦记录下来。
t_en: Last night, I had a long dream about the day we met. I want to write down that dream.

t_ch: 记录下来…是要做什么？
t_en: Write it down... Why?

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 为了让我不要忘记一些事。钟表小子，你还记得自己的名字是怎么来的么？
t_en: So I won't forget it. Do you remember how you got your name, Clockie?

t_ch: 当然记得！你跟我说过，小时候你住在钟表房里，那些挂钟、怀表陪着你长大，是你最好的朋友！
t_en: Of course! You told me that when you were a kid, you lived in a room full of clocks. Those wall clocks and pocket watches grew up with you and were your best friends!

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 是啊，但我没告诉你，这故事背后…还有一场美妙的误会。
t_en: Yes, but what I didn't mention was... there's a funny misunderstanding behind it.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 那时我还小。在我的记忆里，有一块特别的怀表。它总是陪伴在爷爷身边，跟着他出海远航，在每一个冒险故事里为他指明方向。
t_en: I was a kid, and there was always a special pocket watch in my memories. It was with my grandpa, guiding him on his sea voyages and leading the way in his every adventure story.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 我也好想拥有这么一块怀表啊，然后，我的梦里就出现了你。
t_en: I wanted to have a pocket watch like that too, and that's when you appeared in my dreams.

t_ch: 是啊，在每一个夜晚，我们都会登上「罗盘号」，一起扬帆起航！
t_en: Yeah, every night we boarded the Compass and set sail together!

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 可是你知道吗？直到爷爷把它交给我的那天，我才恍然大悟，那其实不是什么怀表……
t_en: But you know what? It wasn't until the day my grandpa gave it to me that I realized... it wasn't a pocket watch at all...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 而是一块「罗盘」。
t_en: It was a compass.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 所以你的名字，应该是「罗盘小子」……
t_en: So, your name should have been "Compassie"...

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 而「钟表匠」…就是「无名客」啊。
t_en: And the Watchmaker... is just a Nameless.

s_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔
t_ch: 拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔，或者更简洁的…{RUBY_B#米哈伊尔-夏尔}米沙{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: Mikhail Char Legwork, or simply... {RUBY_B#Mikhail-Char}Misha{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 如果你们希望，用人们更熟知的名字——「钟表匠」来称呼我，也并无不可。
t_en: If you prefer, you can call me by a more familiar name, the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 原来，你就是「钟表匠」本人？！
t_en: So, you're the Watchmaker himself?

t_ch: 很可惜，那位人人憧憬的美梦大亨早已不在了，我只是他人生的一个缩影。
t_en: Unfortunately, that legendary figure is no more. I'm only a reflection of his life.

t_ch: 而陪伴各位同行至今的这个孩子，是他童年美梦中懵懂无知的主角——「钟表小子」的好朋友，一名年轻的学徒，一位未来的列车机修工……
t_en: As for the child who has been with you, he's the innocent protagonist of Misha's childhood dream, a friend of Clockie, a young apprentice, and a future mechanic on the Express...

t_ch: 同时，也是他一生「开拓」的起点。
t_en: And this also marks the beginning of his journey devoted to the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 行遍人生旅途的最后，我把这一点自视珍贵的火苗留在最深的梦里，希望交给后世的无名客们。
t_en: At the end of the journey, I left this little flame, which I so cherished, in my deepest dreams, hoping to pass it on to the Nameless of future generations.

t_ch: 可不知怎的，他竟然擅自从梦泡里跑了出去，还把使命全都忘光了。抱歉，真是让各位看了一出笑话。
t_en: However, he somehow left the dream bubble and forgot all about his task. I apologize for all the confusion this caused.

t_ch: 因为他生来就想要「开拓」，不是吗？
t_en: Because he was born with a desire to "trailblaze," wasn't he?

t_ch: 我想小米沙也没有忘记身为引导者的使命，所以才会误把自己当作酒店门童，出现在{NICKNAME}入梦的第一刻。
t_en: I don't think Misha has forgotten his role as a guide. He remembered it, and that's why he mistakenly appeared as a hotel {RUBY_B#bellboy}doorman{RUBY_E#} in {NICKNAME}'s dream from the very beginning.

t_ch: 将昏迷中的{NICKNAME}带进这里的，想必也是他吧。如此看来，我们岂不是在最开始就和「『钟表匠』的遗产」擦肩而过了？
t_en: The one who brought our unconscious {NICKNAME} here must have been Misha. If that's the case, we encountered the Watchmaker's Legacy from the beginning, didn't we?

t_ch: 呵呵。我有个损友，总说我一辈子弯弯绕绕，最后又回到了起点…可能这就是每个无名客都要经历的阶段吧。
t_en: Well, I have a sarcastic friend who says I always take big detours and end up back where I started... Perhaps that's what every Nameless has to go through.

t_ch: 但最后你们还是找到了我。言归正传，各位寻到这儿，想来一定也很关心「『钟表匠』的遗产」究竟是什么，我的猎犬应该提到了星核，还有大亨的财富……
t_en: But in the end, you've found me. I'm sure you're all wondering what my legacy is. I believe my hound has mentioned the Stellaron and my wealth...

t_ch: 容我致歉，星核确有其事，但米哈伊尔的财富不过是街谈巷议的传言罢了。
t_en: If I may apologize, the Stellaron part is real. As for my wealth, however, it's nothing more than a baseless rumor.

t_ch: 我在孩提时代告别故乡，踏上「开拓」的旅途，路过一站又一站，最后在阿斯德纳停下。我和朋友建设了最初的匹诺康尼，又为它的未来奋斗至今……
t_en: I left my homeland as a child and embarked on the journey of Trailblaze. I traveled to various planets until finally reaching Asdana, where my friends and I built the original Penacony and fought for its future ever since...

t_ch: 我的一生都在前进，尽己所能冲破那些拦住去路的障碍。但最后，我的路也走到了尽头，身躯就像一节破破烂烂的车头，身后也没留下任何值得托付的财产……
t_en: I've been moving forward all my life, doing what I could to overcome the obstacles in my path. But ultimately, my journey reached its end, and I left behind no possessions worth entrusting...

t_ch: 所以，要问这节破旧的列车头里还剩什么能被称作「遗产」的东西…我想也只有那些依旧还在引擎炉膛里燃烧的事物了。
t_en: So, if you ask what's left within this worn-out train engine that can be called a "legacy"... I suppose it's the things that are still burning in the engine's furnace.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的现状，你们已然知晓。我当然希望有人来帮助这个世界重回正轨。但这个决定应当由你们来完成，因为「开拓」的道路从来不由他人铺就。
t_en: Now that you're well aware of the current situation of Penacony, I certainly hope that you'll help me get this world back on track. But I'll leave that decision to you, for the Path of Trailblaze is never paved by others.

t_ch: 所以，我为各位留下一个故事，和两件礼物——
t_en: All I have for you is a story and two gifts...

t_ch: 我想把它给你们：我的「怀表」。它陪我走过漫长的旅程，指引那个一无所知的傻孩子不断向前，有幸和这么多伟大的人一起走到了今天。
t_en: I want to give you my pocket watch. It has accompanied me throughout my long journey, guiding that naive child forward, and has been blessed with the presence of so many great people up to this day.

t_ch: 还有我的帽子。那个为我领航的人把它扣在我的脑袋上，从此安下一个不切实际的念想：「开拓」之旅永远也不会结束。
t_en: And my hat, too. The one who navigated for me placed it on my head and planted a fanciful thought in my mind — the Trailblazing expedition will never end.

t_ch: 接下来，就该你们作出选择了。如果下定决心，就推开那扇门，走进一位老人长长的梦吧。
t_en: Now, it's time for you to make your choice. Once you've made up your mind, open that door and enter the long dream of an old man.

t_ch: 我会在这条时光走廊的尽头，等候各位的到来。
t_en: I'll be waiting for you at the end of this corridor of time.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#「同谐」希佩}那位星神{RUBY_E#}竟会在这种时候向匹诺康尼投来瞥视…是「开拓」的传承产生共鸣了么？还是说，各位的默契连星神都能打动？
t_en: I can't believe {RUBY_B#Xipe, the Harmony}that Aeon{RUBY_E#} would cast a glance at Penacony at a time like this... Is it because of the resonance from the legacy of the Trailblaze? Or perhaps the bond between you is so strong that it even impresses an Aeon?

t_ch: 在我看来，倒是还有一种可能性——或许祂也想知道匹诺康尼的未来会掌握在谁的手中，才会代{RUBY_B#阿基维利和太一}已死的星神{RUBY_E#}前来见证。
t_en: Well, there might be another possibility. Perhaps THEY want to witness, on behalf of the {RUBY_B#Akivili and Ena}fallen Aeons{RUBY_E#}, who will hold the future of Penacony.

t_ch: 既然如此，我谨代表匹诺康尼的梦主，和橡木家系十万七千三百三十六位同胞向各位正式发出邀请——
t_en: If that's the case, on behalf of the Dreammaster of Penacony and the <unbreak>107,336</unbreak> members of the Oak Family, I'm extending a formal invitation to all of you...

t_ch: 我们诚邀各位莅临匹诺康尼大剧院，参加即将开幕的谐乐大典。当然，各位要登上的不是观众席，而是舞台中央。
t_en: I'm cordially inviting you all to the Penacony Grand Theater to participate in the upcoming Charmony Festival. And of course, you won't be in the audience, but on center stage.

t_ch: 事关星核、匹诺康尼、乃至整个银河的未来。公平起见，就让我们在那里一见真章。
t_en: Since the future of the Stellaron, Penacony, and even the entire Cosmos is at stake, let's draw a conclusion there, in all fairness.

t_ch: 既然各位笃信{RUBY_B#「开拓」的星神}阿基维利{RUBY_E#}的道路，就向我展现祂的勇气和觉悟吧。
t_en: If you truly believe in {RUBY_B#Aeon of Trailblaze}Akivili's{RUBY_E#} path, then show me THEIR courage and determination.

t_ch: 他的意思，是要我们去谐乐大典上一较高下吗？
t_en: Does that mean he wants to fight us during the Charmony Festival?

t_ch: 恐怕就是这个意思。
t_en: I'm afraid so.

t_ch: 好怪啊！我还一直防着大反派什么时候会搞事呢，结果他到最后还在说什么「公平起见」…该不会根本没把咱们放在眼里吧？
t_en: This is weird! Aren't arch villains usually plotting some dirty conspiracy in the end? But he actually said something like "in all fairness"... Could it be that he's underestimating us?

t_ch: 在我看来，星期日对自己的理想深信不疑，也是真心实意想向我们证明「秩序」的正确。
t_en: Well, in my opinion, Sunday is deeply committed to his own philosophy and genuinely wants to prove that the Order is right.

t_ch: 从他身上，我感受到强烈的信念和支配欲，倘若不能堂堂正正地胜出，想必他也没法给自己一个交代吧。
t_en: I sensed a strong conviction and a desire for dominance in him. Maybe he won't accept the outcome unless he wins fair and square.

t_ch: 也正因如此，在接下来的对决中——他必然会全力以赴。
t_en: That's why he'll give it his all in the upcoming battle.

t_ch: 我们也不能退缩。
t_en: We won't back down either.

t_ch: 嗯…你说得对。咱们连「毁灭」的绝灭大君都收拾过了，区区「秩序」肯定也不在话下！
t_en: Yeah, you're right! We've even dealt with a Lord Ravager of the Destruction, so a follower of the Order won't be a big deal!

t_ch: 感觉他好光明磊落…
t_en: But he's such a gentleman. If evil, why so nice?

t_ch: 你、你怎么怂了？！咱们连「毁灭」的绝灭大君都收拾过了，区区「秩序」肯定也不在话下！
t_en: A—Are you backing out now!? We've even dealt with a Lord Ravager of the Destruction, so a follower of the Order won't be a big deal!

t_ch: 刚耍完帅，只能硬着头皮上了…
t_en: I flexed too hard with my swag. Guess I now have to pay my dues, huh...

t_ch: 你…！你这家伙，每次到重要关头就掉链子。
t_en: You...! Every time an important moment arrives, you hesitate.

t_ch: 咱们连「毁灭」的绝灭大君都收拾过了，区区「秩序」肯定也不在话下！
t_en: We've even dealt with a Lord Ravager of the Destruction, so a follower of the Order won't be a big deal!

t_ch: 无论如何，星穹列车不能对星核坐视不理。为匹诺康尼「开拓」未来，也是米哈伊尔等前人的夙愿。
t_en: Anyway, we can't leave the Stellaron unchecked. This is about trailblazing a bright future for Penacony and fulfilling Mikhail's and his predecessors' long-cherished wishes.

t_ch: 各位，我们既然接过了接力棒，就一定不能辜负他们的意志。
t_en: Now that we've taken up the mantle, we can't afford to fail them.

t_ch: 但这对「秩序」而言也是一样的，他们的计划并非一朝一夕，它的背后是「盛会之星」孕育了数百年的庞大意识——
t_en: However, the same applies to the Order. Their plan didn't materialize overnight, and they have the profound collective consciousness of the Planet of Festivities behind them...

t_ch: 想要入梦的渴望，想要沉睡的怠惰，还有逃避、放弃…人们在无形中被催生的情绪，成了「秩序」美梦诞生的摇篮。
t_en: A desire to dream, to slumber and escape reality... All those hidden emotions have given birth to the sweet dream of the Order.

t_ch: 利用一整个世界的意志，推动一位星神的降生…这场对决绝不是单纯力量的交锋。为了匹诺康尼的未来，不能只有你们在舞台上战斗。
t_en: They've harnessed the will of an entire planet to create an Aeon... This confrontation is far more complicated than a simple power struggle. To secure Penacony's future, fighting on the stage alone is not enough.

t_ch: 你们？什么意思，你不跟我们一起走了吗……
t_en: What do you mean? Are you not coming with us...?

t_ch: 我想，流萤小姐的意思是，她要赶赴另一片战场了。
t_en: I believe Firefly is trying to say that she's heading off to another battle.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Mhmm.

t_ch: 这个房间的氛围，好不一样啊。
t_en: The atmosphere in this room feels totally different.

t_ch: 米沙，你终于来啦！
t_en: Misha! You've finally come!

t_ch: 钟表小子，原来你在这。对啊，我们第一次见面，好像也是在这个房间……
t_en: Clockie, you're here! Yeah, this is the room where we first met each other...

t_ch: 这满墙的书籍，就是那位米哈伊尔航海士留下的日志吗？
t_en: Are those books on the bookshelf logbooks left behind by that seafarer?

t_ch: 米哈伊尔每次出海回来，都会把一本航海日志放在房间的书架上。这是他探索世界每一寸角落的记录。
t_en: Yeah, whenever he came back, he'd place a logbook on the bookshelf in his room. They contain records of his expeditions to every corner of the world.

t_ch: 他说，我们的世界就像那座喷泉一样，不知从什么时候开始，海水正在一点一点淹没人们生活的地方。
t_en: He described our world as a fountain. At some point, the sea started to gradually swallow up the land where people lived.

t_ch: 为了让大家有能够安身的土地，米哈伊尔必须不断出海探索，找到海水的源头。
t_en: To ensure that everyone had land to settle on, he had to continue exploring the sea and search for the source of the rising seawater.

t_ch: 那一天，他把我叫到书房里，说他要像往常那样出海了。但我看得出来，爷爷的表情很严肃，爸爸最后一次远航前，我在他脸上见过同样的表情。
t_en: On that day, he called me to his study, telling me that he was embarking on another journey. However, I could sense the gravity in his expression. It... It was the same look I had seen on my father's face before his final voyage.

t_ch: 我恳求他把我带上，他却说我的冒险不在这里，让我留在家中，耐心等待门外的声音。
t_en: I asked him if I could go with him, but he said that my adventure lay elsewhere and told me to stay home and patiently await a certain sound at the door.

t_ch: 什么声音？
t_en: What sound?

t_ch: 米哈伊尔说，天上有更浩瀚的海洋，是星星的海洋。有一辆列车载着想要去远方的孩子，永不停息地穿越星海。
t_en: He told me about a vast ocean in the sky — an ocean of stars. He spoke of a train that transports children with a desire to venture far away, traversing the sea of stars without ever stopping.

t_ch: 他认识那辆车上的人，他会拜托对方带上我一起离开，我梦寐以求的旅行…会从这里开始。
t_en: He said that he knew the crew on the train, and that he had asked them to take me along. He said the journey I had always dreamed of... would start there.

t_ch: 列车？该不会是……
t_en: A train? Could it be...

t_ch: 是「星穹列车」。我想起来了，米哈伊尔的朋友是一群无名客。他们来到我们的世界，为了解决一颗星星引发的灾难。
t_en: It's the Astral Express. I remember now, Grandpa's friends are a group of Nameless who came to this world to resolve a disaster caused by a star.

t_ch: 然后米哈伊尔把自己的怀表给了我。那是他的宝物，出现在每一个扬帆远航的故事里。他说往后的日子不好走，但怀表的指针会为我指明方向。
t_en: Then he gave his pocket watch to me. It was his cherished treasure, appearing in every one of his adventure tales. He explained that difficult times were ahead but assured me that the watch would guide me.

t_ch: 只要不停踏出向前的那一步，我一定能抵达自己想去的地方。然后，我好像就听见了……
t_en: He said, as long as I kept moving forward, I'd eventually reach my desired destination. And then, it was as if I heard...

t_ch: 列车的鸣笛声，它从房间的另一头响起……
t_en: The distant sound of a train whistle, echoing throughout the room...

t_ch: 是的，米沙！然后我们就循着声音的方向去了，对不对？
t_en: Exactly, Misha! And then we followed that whistle, didn't we?

t_ch: 对，我应该还能找到当时的路。
t_en: Yeah, I think I can still find the way we took back then.

t_ch: 今天过后，耶佩拉的名字就会从银河历史中消失，而永火官邸将取而代之，在不远的未来收到一封邀请函……
t_en: After today, Jepella's name will disappear from cosmic history, and the Ever-Flame Mansion will take its place. In the not-too-distant future, you'll receive an invitation...

t_ch: 那就是你的下一站。
t_en: That's your next stop.

t_ch: 梦想之地，匹诺康尼。
t_en: Land of the Dreams, Penacony.

t_ch: 祝你在那里找到想要的答案…或者，解脱。
t_en: I hope you find whatever you seek there, be it answers... or salvation.

t_ch: 你说的…是那三次「死亡」吗？
t_en: You mean... my three "deaths"?

t_ch: 是银狼告诉我的。我只是遗憾它们不在我的剧本里。
t_en: Silver Wolf told me about it. It's such a shame that it's not part of my script.

t_ch: 我想要活下去，但我不害怕死亡。死亡的反面是永生，那从来…不是我的所求。
t_en: While I want to live, I'm never afraid of death. The opposite of death is eternal life, and that's... that's something I'll never desire.

t_ch: 人终有一死，我也一样。死亡就像剧本，是无法违抗的命运。但也正因如此……
t_en: People die, and I am no exception. Death is like a script, a fate that cannot be defied. But that's exactly why...

t_ch: 我们才要为自己选择埋骨之地。
t_en: We have to choose where we want to rest forever.

t_ch: 你的生存，是为了灭亡？
t_en: Do you exist just to perish?

t_ch: 你不也一样吗？刃，你渴望的终结…从来不是由他人定义的。
t_en: Are you not the same, Blade? The end you desire is not one dictated by others.

t_ch: 如果现在死去，我就只是一件「兵器」。但我想…我应该要以一个「人」的身份死去。
t_en: If I were to die now, I would only be a weapon. I believe... I should die as a human.

t_ch: 尽管它的定义离我还很遥远，可普通人终其一生寻找的，不也是这么一个答案吗？一个能在墓志铭里留下的…短短的名字。
t_en: Though its definition escapes me, isn't this the answer that ordinary people look for their whole lives? A name that can be carved onto their tombstone.

t_ch: 属于我的那一块，它曾经刻着「格拉默铁骑」，如今刻着「星核猎手」，而总有一天……
t_en: The tombstone that belongs to me once bore the inscription "Glamoth's Iron Cavalry," then it changed to "Stellaron Hunter," but one day...

t_ch: 它会写下「流萤」的名字，和她在生命尽头绽放的华彩。
t_en: It will bear the name "Firefly" and the brilliance she showed at the end of her life.

t_ch: …出发前，「命运的奴隶」告诉我，此行会让我得到难以忘怀的收获。他给出的剧本只有寥寥数行，却让人难以忽视。
t_en: ...Before I left, Destiny's Slave told me that this journey would bring unforgettable rewards. Even though the script he gave me only had a few lines, I couldn't ignore them.

t_ch: 因为其中一行写着…我会在梦想之地经历三次「死亡」。
t_en: Because one of the lines said... I'll experience "death" three times in the land of the dreams.

t_ch: 三、三次死亡？！这一定是打引号的吧……
t_en: Three times!? This can't be serious, right...?

t_ch: 第一次是如同死亡般的痛苦，我的身体被「沉眠」的翼刃贯穿，才有了后来所有的故事。剧本必定会应验，但形式…只在翻开那一页时才会显露。
t_en: The first time was a painful death when I was stabbed by the blade of "Dormancy," which led to all subsequent events. The script will always come true, but in a way... that will only be revealed when that page is turned.

t_ch: 所以现在，我已经理解了第二次「死亡」的含义，并要将它付诸行动。如果一切顺利，这会为你们提供至关重要的支援。
t_en: So now, I've understood the meaning of my second "death," and I am prepared to face it. If all goes well, my efforts will provide crucial support for you.

t_ch: 只有赢得这场胜利，匹诺康尼才有未来可言。也唯有如此，那尚未到来的第三次，也是最后一次死亡…才不会以最糟糕的样子呈现。
t_en: Only by achieving victory in this battle can we secure a future for Penacony. And only then, my third and final death... won't come true in the most terrible form.

t_ch: 最糟糕的样子，那不就是……
t_en: The most terrible form? Does that mean...?

t_ch: 真正的「死亡」…匹诺康尼的所有人都会在「秩序」的美梦中永远沉沦。
t_en: The true "death"... where everyone in Penacony loses themselves completely in the eternal sweet dream of the Order.

t_ch: 那是我们无论如何都要避免的未来。
t_en: We must do everything we can to prevent that.

t_ch: 流萤小姐，你…已经做好觉悟了吗？
t_en: Have you... made up your mind, Firefly?

t_ch: 嗯。如果没有，我就不会来到这里。
t_en: Yes, otherwise I wouldn't be here.

t_ch: 再次感谢你对星穹列车提供的帮助。祝愿我们在现实中再见。
t_en: Thank you again for your assistance for the Astral Express. May we meet again in reality.

t_ch: 嗯，再见，各位。愿你们的「开拓」之旅——永不终结。
t_en: Yeah, farewell, everyone. May your Trailblazing expedition never end.

t_ch: 与此同时，流梦礁的某处……
t_en: In the meantime... somewhere at Dreamflux Reef...

t_ch: 没想到啊，老头，你那没头没脑的计划真成了。难道你们无名客全都是些只会意气用事的傻瓜么？
t_en: That's quite unexpected, old man. Who would've thought your crazy plan would actually work? Do all you Nameless fools just act on a whim?

t_ch: 我能嗅到，虚假的美梦就要结束了。那群无名客虽然年轻，但确实有能力做到这件事…就像你们当年那样。
t_en: I can sense that this false sweet dream is coming to an end. The Nameless may be young, but they had the ability to achieve this goal... just like you did in your time.

t_ch: 可惜啊，没能让你亲眼见证这一幕。恐怕我也没这个福分了…「虚构」的事物被看穿，也就相当于不存在了。
t_en: It's a shame you won't be able to see it firsthand. Heh, maybe I won't either... Once something {RUBY_B#Fictionologist}fictional{RUBY_E#} is seen through, it ceases to exist.

t_ch: 哼，不谈那群无名客，那头上长翅膀的小子也跟你一模一样，死心眼儿，不见棺材不落泪…天意弄人啊，要不是这该死的命途，咱几个没准真能聊到一起去。
t_en: Yeah, not just those Nameless, even Mr. Wings is just like you... Stubborn, won't listen or give up, no matter what... Well, fate is unpredictable, I guess. If we weren't bound by those cursed Paths, maybe we could've had some good talks.

t_ch: 不过，咱最后到底是狠狠出了口恶气。这下舒服了。还记得那帮混蛋当年是怎么咒咱们的吗，嗯？他们说：「下地狱去吧，该死的叛徒」……
t_en: But in the end, we managed to do it, and now we can find solace. Remember how those idiots cursed us? They said, "Go to hell, you worthless traitors!" Heh...

t_ch: 米哈伊尔啊，米哈伊尔，我不知道他们说的是不是真的，但如果心向自由就活该要下地狱……
t_en: Well, I don't know if they really meant it, but if longing for freedom means going to hell...

t_ch: 那我很快就要下去找你了，老东西。
t_en: Then I'll be joining you soon, you old fool.

t_ch: 让我们在地狱里再次共进晚餐吧……
t_en: Let's get together and have supper again in hell...

t_ch: 哦，差点忘记了，还有件事……
t_en: Oh, I almost forgot. There's one more thing...

t_ch: 用这杯「聚散有时」向你致意…{NICKNAME}……
t_en: Here's to you, a glass of "Hello and Goodbye"... {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 敬不完美的…明天。
t_en: To the imperfect... tomorrow.

t_ch: 真暖和啊。这死海边平时连个活物都见不着，你倒是幸运，找到了避雨的地方不说，还有新鲜浆果，真不容易。
t_en: It's warm here, isn't it? You're lucky to have found shelter from the rain, let alone fresh berries, in this desolate place.

t_ch: 只是循着「生命」的气息来了。在这种地方，这气息格外分明。
t_en: I was just following the scent of life. It's particularly strong in a place like this.

t_ch: 只可惜，尝起来实在是有点淡。
t_en: It's a shame these berries don't have much flavor.

t_ch: 真的？你可能不知道，这种果实倒也算得上多汁，唯一的问题只是放在口中咀嚼时……
t_en: Seriously? In case you didn't know, this fruit is pretty juicy. The only downside is that when you chew it...

t_ch: …会产生极其辛辣的刺激。
t_en: ...It becomes extremely spicy.

t_ch: 你…没有味觉了么？
t_en: Have you... lost your sense of taste?

t_ch: 有些味道还是能尝见的…比如微微的「甜」。
t_en: I can still taste certain things... like a faint sweetness.

t_ch: 来到这里前，我的上一站叫俄尔刻龙。那里也有荒无人烟的山崖，也有火堆照亮的夜晚。天上会下紫红色的雪，含在嘴里…有树莓的味道。
t_en: Before coming here, I stopped by a place called Orkron. It had barren cliffs and nights lit by bonfires. Burgundy snow would fall from the sky, and when it landed on my tongue... it tasted like raspberries.

t_ch: 那味道算不上甜美，却令人记忆犹新。每当我回首时，总会发现串联起来时路的…不是刻骨铭心的起承转合，而是这么一个个难忘的瞬间。
t_en: The flavor wasn't exactly sweet, but it left a lasting impression. When I think back on my past, I realize that what's tying everything together... isn't the big events, but rather these small yet unforgettable moments.

t_ch: 别在意。逐渐丧失自我的存在…是每个自灭者都要面对的现实。至少，我还没有完全失去感官和记忆。
t_en: Don't worry about it. Losing oneself is a reality that every Self-Annihilator must face. At least, I haven't completely lost my senses and memories yet.

t_ch: 那就祝贺你又为旅途添上了新的注脚吧。
t_en: Well, congratulations on adding another footnote to your journey.

t_ch: 话说回来，你一个人？
t_en: By the way, are you always alone?

t_ch: 不，我在俄尔刻龙还有个同伴。她个头小小的，是个无名客，想把自己发射到「IX」里去…她总说自己要走一条「比阿基维利更深、更远的路」。
t_en: No, I had a companion in Orkron. She's a short Nameless girl who aspired to explore IX... She always said she'd walk a path "deeper and farther than Akivili's."

t_ch: 个子不大，野心不小…那结果呢？
t_en: Quite an ambition for such a small girl... So, what happened?

t_ch: 她…变成了一潭死水。
t_en: She... became stagnant water.

t_ch: 呵…节哀。
t_en: Well... my condolences.

t_ch: 哀伤么？我不这么认为。那女孩是笑着离开的，她从未对自己的选择感到后悔，也一定希望我能笑着和她道别…我的确是这么做的。
t_en: Condolences? I don't need them. The girl left with a smile. She never regretted her choice and most definitely wanted me to say goodbye with a smile... So that's what I did.

t_ch: 这就是你在为她感到悲伤的证明。
t_en: That's proof that you're grieving for her.

t_ch: 或许是害怕呢？
t_en: Or perhaps I'm just afraid?

t_ch: 害怕？我很难从你身上感受到这种情绪。你怕什么？
t_en: Afraid? I rarely sense that emotion from you. What do you fear?

t_ch: 我怕会忘记和她一起走过的三十天，就像我生命中的每个三十一天。它们中的大部分已经同雨水一起逝去，消失在看不见的彼岸。
t_en: I'm afraid I'll forget the thirty days I spent with her, just like all the other days in my life. Most of them have already washed away with the rain, disappearing into an unseen realm.

t_ch: 我怕这些鲜红的记忆也离开我。我能看到的颜色已经不多了，除了这一点淡淡的、温暖的「红」，我几乎一无所有。
t_en: I fear that those vivid red memories will fade away too. There isn't much color left, and besides this faint, warm red, there's almost nothing.

t_ch: 真是难以想象…一个看惯了鲜血、破灭和混乱的「游侠」居然能从红色里看出温暖。
t_en: Hard to imagine... a "Ranger" accustomed to bloodshed, destruction, and chaos finding warmth in the red color.

t_ch: 因为这样的温暖，我也拥有过许多。很久以前，我和他人约定过，要把它带给更多的人，在余生的每一刻都去追寻…「对所有人都更好的结局」。
t_en: Because I have experienced this warmth many times. Long ago, I promised someone that I'd bring it to more people, and that for every remaining moment of my life, I'd strive for... a better ending for all.

t_ch: 只要这一抹「红色」尚在，我就还有机会履行约定。它可以是燃烧的火，是盛放的花，是这岩洞里的一丛浆果…它就是生命本身，转瞬即逝、却足够夺目。
t_en: As long as this red color still lingers, I have a chance to fulfill that promise. It represents a burning fire, a blooming flower, the berries in this cave... and life itself, fleeting yet still dazzling.

t_ch: 最后，它会引领我跨越「存在的地平线」，在彼岸的尽头…斩断「虚无」。
t_en: In the end, it will lead me beyond the Horizon of Existence, and on the other side, I will... cut off Nihility.

t_ch: 身受沉眠无相者的祝福，却想着要如何杀死祂？这可真是…彻头彻尾的「虚无」啊。
t_en: The one blessed by the sleeping and shapeless is considering how to kill THEM? That's truly... pure Nihility.

t_ch: 不过，有一点你说得对。在这阴雨绵绵的死水边待久了，只有望着这团鲜红的火时，我才发觉自己原来还活着。
t_en: But you're right about one thing — After spending so much time near this stagnant water, only when I look at this vibrant red fire do I realize that I'm still alive.

t_ch: 雨啊…什么时候才会停呢？
t_en: When will this rain... ever stop?

t_ch: 也许…等亡者的怨念悉数平息，天就放晴了吧。
t_en: Perhaps... when the sorrows of the departed have finally quieted down, the sky will clear up.

t_ch: 丹恒先生，你听说过比亚里-斯卡曼德洛斯星么？那是「同谐」影响下的地上天国之一，大小达耳达努星系居民们趋之若鹜的人间天堂。
t_en: Have you heard of a planet named Byari Skamandros, Dan Heng? It's one of the paradises under the influence of the Harmony, a sought-after wonderland for the inhabitants of the Dardanu Major and Minor systems.

t_ch: 半个琥珀纪前，家族在那里举办了一场空前绝后的庆典，而宴会过后…星球上的每个人都成了「家族」的一员。
t_en: Half an Amber Era ago, The Family held an unprecedented festival there, and after that... everyone on the planet became part of The Family.

t_ch: 你认为同样的事会发生在匹诺康尼？
t_en: Do you think the same thing will happen on Penacony?

t_ch: 不然要如何解释呢？家族特意借「钟表匠」的邀请让一众命途行者滞留其中，却唯独放逐了「虚无」的令使……
t_en: Yes, or how else can we explain it? The Family deliberately used the Watchmaker's invitation to keep all the Pathstriders here but banished the Emanator of the Nihility...

t_ch: 受「虚无」影响，我很难受到其他命途力量的影响，反倒能无意识地侵染它们…这或许就是他们不想看到的「变量」。
t_en: Because of the Nihility, I'm rarely affected by the power of other Paths, but somehow I can unconsciously infiltrate them... Maybe that's the "risk" they're trying to avoid.
